mechanism_narrative: Sustained low nominal interest rates reduce the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly compressing valuation multiples and generating positive forward S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 6.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and impending recession, causing investors to price in falling corporate earnings and producing negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: UNRATE exceeds 6.0 percent but 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad consumer demand contraction, reducing corporate revenue expectations and driving negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST do not predict negative 20-day forward returns, with Rank IC falling below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects robust supply-side momentum that supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day forward returns during tightening periods, with a win rate below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 2.5 percent triggers expectations of immediate Federal Reserve tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5 percent but 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates labor force participation deterioration, signaling structural economic weakness that depresses forward equity returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS and UNRATE moving in the same direction does not predict negative 60-day forward returns, with Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter GDP growth above 2.0 percent reflects sustained aggregate demand expansion that directly supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0 percent but 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index exceeding 0.5 percent signals immediate supply-side contraction and inventory drawdowns, triggering negative equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines exceed 0.5 percent but 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of triggered instances.
